subject: Public Health
title: Defining Public Health
description: A fast breakdown of Defining Public Health for builders.

hook.text: Medicine saves one patient at a time. Public health tries to save entire populations.

buildup.text: It focuses on prevention—keeping people healthy before they ever need a doctor.

discovery.text: Clean water, vaccines, seatbelts, and food safety are all public health victories.

twist.text: The greatest gains in life expectancy came not from hospitals but from sanitation and hygiene.

climax.text: Public health added 30 years to average life expectancy in the 20th century alone.

punchline.text: The best medicine is the disease you never get.

quiz.question: What contributed most to increased life expectancy in the 20th century?
